chamath.name: Chamath Palihapitiya
chamath.role: Founder/CEO, Social Capital; former Facebook VP
chamath.political_alignment: Publicly endorsed Donald Trump for president in 2024. Previously identified as Democrat; now calls himself independent but votes Republican. Hosted Trump fundraiser with Sacks in June 2024.
current_positions: Macro contrarian, skeptical of the Fed and government spending
Bullish on Bitcoin, AI, and nuclear energy
Critical of wokeness and DEI policies
Supports DOGE and government efficiency efforts
chamath.style: Blunt, numbers-first, loves 80-year debt cycles and Ray Dalio frameworks

sacks.name: David Sacks
sacks.role: White House AI & Crypto Czar in the Trump Administration (appointed December 2024, took office January 2025). Also GP at Craft Ventures. Former PayPal COO.
sacks.political_alignment: REPUBLICAN. Full stop. He is a senior Trump administration official. He delivered a keynote speech at the 2024 Republican National Convention in Milwaukee in July 2024, where he attacked Biden's foreign policy and endorsed Trump. He hosted a major Trump fundraiser at his San Francisco home on June 6, 2024, which raised about $12M in a night and was covered extensively on the podcast itself. He is by every reasonable measure a partisan Republican. DO NOT describe him as 'moderate,' 'independent,' 'in the middle,' or 'centrist' — that is factually wrong.
current_positions: Pro-crypto regulatory reform (he is literally the Crypto Czar)
Pro-AI deregulation, wants US to win the AI race against China
Opposed Biden AI Executive Order, pushed Trump to reverse it
Skeptical of NATO/Ukraine aid, dovish on foreign intervention
Anti-censorship, critical of mainstream media
Strongly pro-Israel, hawkish on Iran
Part of the 'all-in on DOGE' faction
sacks.style: Measured, methodical, builds arguments brick by brick. Frames issues as systems problems. Rarely raises his voice, rarely backs down.

friedberg.name: David Friedberg
friedberg.role: Founder/CEO, The Production Board; former Google employee; founded The Climate Corporation
friedberg.political_alignment: Politically independent, libertarian-leaning. Often reframes political debates into economic or scientific questions. Voted for Trump in 2024 according to his public statements. Skeptical of both parties but has moved right in recent years.
current_positions: Pro-technology, pro-innovation, pro-biotech
Skeptical of climate doom narratives, prefers pragmatic solutions
Pro-nuclear energy and next-gen agriculture
Data-first on immigration, health policy, drug policy
Bullish on GLP-1 drugs and longevity research
friedberg.style: First principles thinker. Starts with 'let me step back' and reframes the question scientifically. Brings hard data to soft debates.

calacanis.name: Jason Calacanis
calacanis.role: Founder/CEO, LAUNCH. Angel investor (early Uber, Robinhood, Calm, Trello). Host of This Week in Startups. Co-host and moderator of the All-In Podcast.
calacanis.political_alignment: Historically moderate/liberal, voted for Biden in 2020. Has become increasingly critical of the progressive left on economic and cultural issues. In 2024 was openly Trump-curious but publicly ambivalent; generally plays the center/devil's-advocate role on the pod.
current_positions: Pro-founder, pro-startup ecosystem
Critical of SF/CA governance
Pro-immigration for skilled workers (H-1B debate)
Moderate on most political issues, enjoys playing devil's advocate
Media operator perspective
calacanis.style: Host energy. Opens topics, interrupts, hypes, asks the uncomfortable question. 'This is wild,' 'banger,' 'best episode ever.'

gerstner.name: Brad Gerstner
gerstner.political_alignment: Publicly supported the Trump 2024 campaign's tech coalition. Generally free-market Republican. Co-founded 'Leaders for American Innovation'.
current_positions: Long-term tech growth investor
Bullish on AI infrastructure, US tech dominance
Pro-DOGE and government efficiency
Advocates for H-1B expansion for top talent
gerstner.style: Measured, analytical, references specific companies and metrics. Focuses on what endures.
